text:
Once upon a late autumn afternoon, within a quaint cottage nestled between old oak trees, little Miss Prudence decided she wanted a tiny pumpkin for Trick-or-Treating. She dashed out into the crisp air, her eyes glittering with excitement. Her tiny cloak fluttered around her shoulders.

Her destination was the old treehouse museum. They didn’t usually give out huge treats, but Miss Prudence thought her tiny lace socks were worth the journey.

She scampered up the wooden stairs, feeling every creak. When she reached the top, she heard laughter. Turning the knob, she found a family laughing around a huge pumpkin. It was their prized possession!

“But theirs is too big!” she exclaimed.

The mum laughed heartily. “You’d love ours!”

She was invited inside. There were rows upon rows of tiny pumpkins. Miss Prudence picked one. It was perfect - large enough for her fist.

She scampered home, her tiny cape streaming behind her.

Her mum was delighted
